abstract.p: This topic demonstrates all supported DITA elements rendered as Google Docs content.
abstract.shortdesc: Each section below showcases a different element type with its expected visual output.

ul.li:
Top-level item
Second-level item
Third-level item
Another second-level item

ol.li:
First step
Sub-step A
Sub-step B

ul.li:
Bullet item with numbered sub-items:
Numbered child one
Numbered child two

example.title: Example: Running the Plugin
example.p: The following command converts a DITA map to a Google Doc:
The output URL is printed to standard output.

section.title: Screen Output
section.p: Terminal output is rendered in monospace font:
section.screen: $ dita --input=sample.ditamap --format=googledoc [INFO] Processing sample.ditamap [INFO] Created document: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1abc.../edit BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 8s

section.title: Long Quote
section.p: The following is a quotation from the DITA specification:
section.lq: DITA maps are documents that collect and organize references to DITA topics to indicate the relationships among the topics. They can also serve as outlines or tables of contents for DITA deliverables and as build manifests for DITA projects.
section.p: Long quotes are rendered with italic text and indentation.

section.title: Footnotes
section.p: The plugin supports inline footnotesFootnotes are rendered as inline bracketed text rather than true Google Docs footnotes. that appear directly in the text flow. Multiple footnotesEach footnote is numbered sequentially within the document. are supported.

messagepanel.consequence: Contact with live circuits can cause severe injury or death.
messagepanel.howtoavoid: Disconnect all power sources before opening the equipment enclosure.

messagepanel.consequence: Unsaved changes will be lost if the service is restarted.
messagepanel.howtoavoid: Save all work before performing maintenance operations.

dlentry.dd: Darwin Information Typing Architecture. An XML-based standard for authoring, producing, and delivering technical documentation.
DITA Open Toolkit. The reference implementation of the DITA specification, providing transforms to various output formats.
A file format used to filter and flag DITA content based on conditional processing attributes such as audience, platform, and product.

conbody.note: Use --filter=conditions.ditaval to apply DITAVAL filtering during the transformation.
The Google Docs API has rate limits. For large documents, the plugin automatically batches requests and retries on throttling errors.
